action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 16 training completed (no crash), but loss is NaN and val AP only 0.024. Eval submission crashed because predict.py used weights_only=True on checkpoint containing numpy arrays. Analysis: Low AP indicates graph-level aggregated features are insufficient; NaN loss suggests numerical issue (likely double-standardization causing extreme values). Evaluation obstruction requires predict.py fix to load with weights_only=False. Decision: Fix predict.py (weights_only=False), correct train.py (remove redundant normalization, add logit clamping and debugging), retrain FeatureMLP for 20 epochs to benchmark. If AP still <0.05, pivot to GNN on CPU subset to capture graph structure. Submit eval immediately after training to get first valid AP score and measure iteration time.
